your_book:
Jaleel's book closed June 30 at $19,804.57, sitting on a $735 unrealized loss (−3.58%) with essentially no cash cushion ($2.49). Today was a broad red session for the portfolio — nearly every holding slipped — with two notable exceptions: Rocket Lab (RKLB) surged +3.71% and NVIDIA (NVDA) gained +2.63%, the only names swimming against the current. The damage was led by Uber (UBER) at −4.42%, Abbott (ABT) at −2.12%, Zoetis (ZTS) at −1.90%, and Boston Scientific (BSX) at −1.84%. The top two weights, Adobe ($3,211 at 16.2%) and ServiceNow ($2,779 at 14%), each shed roughly −0.69% — modest in isolation, but meaningful given their combined 30%+ share of the book.

The thesis-versus-price divergence is sharpest in ADBE and BSX. Adobe carries a −16.4% unrealized loss despite a Strong Buy verdict and QARP of 77.5 — the market is penalizing AI-disruption narrative over fundamentals that actually beat and raised in June. That's a widening gap Jaleel needs to decide is an opportunity or a warning. BSX is similar: down −10.9% with guidance slashed (FY26 growth cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5%) and management signaling flat revenue through Q3, yet still rated Strong Buy. LULU (−10.7%, guidance cut with Americas comps −5% for a fifth straight quarter) and ZTS (−8.8%) are also drifting meaningfully below entry. Meanwhile, RKLB remains flagged AVOID with a QARP of just 36.8 — it's the day's top performer but remains the most structurally misaligned name in a capital-preservation book.

Key catalysts to watch: ABT reports ~July 17, NOW and BSX both ~July 23, NVDA ~August 27, and ADBE ~September 11. A notable options alert on KO — a sweep of Jul 17 $83 calls (2,061 contracts vs. 362 open interest, near the ask) ahead of its July 28 earnings — is worth monitoring given the positioning signal, even though KO is only a $163 position. The book is concentrated in quality names under pressure; the near-zero cash means Jaleel has no dry powder to average down or rebalance without selling something first.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated at 6.7% weight — largest misalignment in the book
attention.note: RKLB is flagged AVOID with a QARP of 36.8, yet it's Jaleel's fifth-largest position by weight ($1,321); a capital-preservation mandate and a speculative space-launch name at that size is a structural contradiction that deserves an explicit hold-or-exit decision, not drift.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, flat through Q3, yet −10.9% and no cash to average
attention.note: FY26 growth was cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat revenue through Q3; the July 23 earnings print is the next hard test and the position is already deep in the red with no cushion to add.

attention.headline: −16.4% loss widening despite beat-and-raise — narrative risk overwhelming fundamentals
attention.note: Adobe beat and raised in June yet the stock continues to fall on AI-moat fears and CFO departure; at 16.2% of the book this is the single largest drag and Jaleel needs a clear line in the sand before the September 11 earnings.

attention.headline: Fifth straight quarter of negative Americas comps — thesis erosion, not a dip
attention.note: The FY guidance cut on June 4, tariff margin pressure, and stalled China growth are not one-off headwinds; five consecutive negative comp quarters signals a structural slowdown, and the next print (~Sep 4) needs to show a reversal or the thesis is broken.
